findings.title: Fork-evidence route artifacts can be lifted into live swaps with a one-wei floor
findings.details: The generated solver routes include complete executable route objects, ABI-encoded calldata, route hashes, real Base targets, and descriptions such as "Composed production route", but the compiler hardcodes final and downstream leg minimums to 1 for most multi-leg paths and uses a deterministic deadline in year 2100. Concrete chain: a release script, frontend importer, or operator consumes base-v1-solver-routes.json because it contains route.route, abiEncodedRoute, callValue, and production-looking descriptions; the route is submitted on mainnet after prices move or under sandwich pressure; executeRoute accepts any output above 1 wei after fee, so the user can lose effectively the whole input while all router checks pass. The status string says generated-fork-evidence, but the artifact shape is directly executable and the compiler enforces the smoke-minimum policy instead of preventing production reuse.
evidence: router-ts/src/compiler/compileRoute.ts:206-246 builds generated routes with real tokens/targets and minAmountOutAfterFee set to 1, including routes described as production composed routes.

router-ts/src/compiler/compileRoute.ts:639-643 rejects generated routes whose final minimum is greater than postFeeMinimum(1), which turns the 1-wei smoke floor into a compiler invariant rather than a clearly non-production placeholder.

router-ts/src/compiler/types.ts:5 sets DETERMINISTIC_DEADLINE to 4102444800, and test/router/fixtures/base-v1-solver-routes.json exposes complete route objects plus abiEncodedRoute/callValue for those long-lived, low-minimum routes.
findings.suggested_fix: Separate fork-evidence artifacts from any executable production route export. Either omit abiEncodedRoute/callValue and use impossible recipients/deadlines for evidence fixtures, or add a production compiler path that requires quote-derived per-leg and final minimums plus a near-term deadline before artifacts can be consumed outside tests.

findings.title: Any non-payable fee recipient bricks all native-output routes after the swaps execute
findings.details: Native-output routes pay the protocol fee in native ETH before transferring net output to the user. Concrete chain: governance or deployment config sets feeRecipient to a contract without a payable receive path; a FAME -> WETH -> ETH or V4 ETH-output route produces native ETH; gross and final minimum checks pass; _transferAsset tries SafeTransferLib.safeTransferETH(feeRecipient, feeAmount); the fee recipient rejects ETH and the whole route reverts. The user funds are reverted, but every native-output path is unavailable until governance changes the fee recipient or fee policy. Current validation only compares the configured address and does not prove that it can receive native fees.
evidence: src/FameRouter.sol:121-124 transfers feeAmount to feeRecipient before final output settlement whenever tokenOut is native ETH and feePpm is nonzero.
src/FameRouter.sol:145-148 accepts any nonzero feeRecipient without checking native ETH receive behavior, and src/FameRouter.sol:386-389 uses reverting safeTransferETH for native transfers.
script/ValidateFameRouterBase.s.sol:71-84 validates feeRecipient by equality only; it does not execute or probe a native-fee route against the configured recipient.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a deployment/validation gate that proves the configured fee recipient can receive native ETH, or route native fees through WETH/claimable accounting so a non-payable recipient cannot brick native-output swaps.

findings.title: Empty-hookData V4 pools bypass the hook allowlist entirely
findings.details: The V4 guard only checks v4HookDataHashEnabled when hookData is non-empty, but a V4 pool can have a nonzero hook address with empty swap hookData. Concrete chain: governance enables the Universal Router target to support the checked-in basedflick/ZORA hooked pool; an attacker submits a route for a different V4 pool with a hostile hook address and hookData = 0x; v4HookDataKey returns hasHookData=false, so no hook/pool key allowlist is consulted; Universal Router executes the hook callbacks under the FameRouter call. The router remains non-reentrant, but the intended hook governance boundary is not actually a hook boundary, it is only a non-empty-bytes boundary.
evidence: src/FameRouter.sol:301-306 only enforces v4HookDataHashEnabled when UniversalRouterAdapter.v4HookDataKey reports hasHookData.

src/router/adapters/UniversalRouterAdapter.sol:182-186 returns hasHookData=false immediately for empty hookData, even though the decoded payload also contains payload.hooks, currency0, currency1, fee, and tickSpacing.

src/router/adapters/UniversalRouterAdapter.sol:163-172 validates token direction and fee/tickSpacing but does not require the V4 pool key or hook address to be allowlisted; test/router/fixtures/base-v1-pools.json includes a nonzero-hook V4 pool with hookData set to 0x.
findings.suggested_fix: Treat nonzero V4 hook addresses as governed even when hookData is empty. Require an allowlisted pool-key or hook-key for every V4 payload with payload.hooks != address(0), and have deploy validation assert every generated V4 route's required key is enabled on the deployed router.

residual_risks: I did not execute Foundry or fork tests because the review was requested as an artifact-only adversarial pass and test runs would create additional build/cache mutations.
The Universal Router Permit2 approval lifetime appears intentionally same-transaction scoped, but this pass did not verify behavior against the live Permit2 bytecode.

The router is publicly callable and intentionally allows user-supplied routes over enabled venue targets; remaining risk depends on whether product intends it as a constrained FAME route executor or a generic fee-taking router.
testing_gaps: No validation test proves the configured fee recipient can receive native ETH fees on a native-output route.
No invariant asserts generated fork-evidence routes are impossible to confuse with production executable artifacts, or that production artifacts must have quote-derived minimums and short deadlines.
No negative V4 test covers a nonzero hook address with empty hookData and an unapproved pool/hook key.
